top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Locating and Editing Factual Associations in GPT
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: We analyze the storage and recall of factual associations in autoregressive transformer language models, finding evidence that these associations correspond to localized, directly-editable computations. We first develop a causal intervention for identifying neuron activations that are decisive in a model's factual pred

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: "Why Should I Trust You?"
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Despite widespread adoption, machine learning models remain mostly black boxes. Understanding the reasons behind predictions is, however, quite important in assessing trust, which is fundamental if one plans to take action based on a prediction, or when choosing whether to deploy a new model. Such understanding also pr

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens, and show that it is possible to train state-of-the-art models using publicly available datasets exclusively, without resorting to proprietary and inaccessible datasets. In partic

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Interpretability in the Wild: a Circuit for Indirect Object Identification in GPT-2 small
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Research in mechanistic interpretability seeks to explain behaviors of machine learning models in terms of their internal components. However, most previous work either focuses on simple behaviors in small models, or describes complicated behaviors in larger models with broad strokes. In this work, we bridge this gap b

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Axiomatic Attribution for Deep Networks
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: We study the problem of attributing the prediction of a deep network to its input features, a problem previously studied by several other works. We identify two fundamental axioms---Sensitivity and Implementation Invariance that attribution methods ought to satisfy. We show that they are not satisfied by most known att

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: In-context Learning and Induction Heads
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: "Induction heads" are attention heads that implement a simple algorithm to complete token sequences like [A][B] ... [A] -> [B]. In this work, we present preliminary and indirect evidence for a hypothesis that induction heads might constitute the mechanism for the majority of all "in-context learning" in large transf

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: BERT Rediscovers the Classical NLP Pipeline
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Pre-trained text encoders have rapidly advanced the state of the art on many NLP tasks. We focus on one such model, BERT, and aim to quantify where linguistic information is captured within the network. We find that the model represents the steps of the traditional NLP pipeline in an interpretable and localizable way,

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Emergent World Representations: Exploring a Sequence Model Trained on a Synthetic Task
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Language models show a surprising range of capabilities, but the source of their apparent competence is unclear. Do these networks just memorize a collection of surface statistics, or do they rely on internal representations of the process that generates the sequences they see? We investigate this question by applying

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Build Predictions by Promoting Concepts in the Vocabulary Space
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Transformer-based language models (LMs) are at the core of modern NLP, but their internal prediction construction process is opaque and largely not understood. In this work, we make a substantial step towards unveiling this underlying prediction process, by reverse-engineering the operation of the feed-forward network

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Knowledge Neurons in Pretrained Transformers
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Large-scale pretrained language models are surprisingly good at recalling factual knowledge presented in the training corpus In this paper, we present preliminary studies on how factual knowledge is stored in pretrained Transformers by introducing the concept of knowledge neurons. Specifically, we examine the fill-in-t

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Sparse Autoencoders Find Highly Interpretable Features in Language Models
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: One of the roadblocks to a better understanding of neural networks' internals is \textit{polysemanticity}, where neurons appear to activate in multiple, semantically distinct contexts. Polysemanticity prevents us from identifying concise, human-understandable explanations for what neural networks are doing internally.

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Progress measures for grokking via mechanistic interpretability
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Neural networks often exhibit emergent behavior, where qualitatively new capabilities arise from scaling up the amount of parameters, training data, or training steps. One approach to understanding emergence is to find continuous \textit{progress measures} that underlie the seemingly discontinuous qualitative changes.

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Analyzing Transformers in Embedding Space
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Understanding Transformer-based models has attracted significant attention, as they lie at the heart of recent technological advances across machine learning. While most interpretability methods rely on running models over inputs, recent work has shown that a zero-pass approach, where parameters are interpreted directl

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Toy Models of Superposition
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Neural networks often pack many unrelated concepts into a single neuron - a puzzling phenomenon known as 'polysemanticity' which makes interpretability much more challenging. This paper provides a toy model where polysemanticity can be fully understood, arising as a result of models storing additional sparse features i

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Mass-Editing Memory in a Transformer
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Recent work has shown exciting promise in updating large language models with new memories, so as to replace obsolete information or add specialized knowledge. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Analyzing Multi-Head Self-Attention: Specialized Heads Do the Heavy Lifting, the Rest Can Be Pruned
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Multi-head self-attention is a key component of the Transformer, a state-of-the-art architecture for neural machine translation. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Towards A Rigorous Science of Interpretable Machine Learning
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: As machine learning systems become ubiquitous, there has been a surge of interest in interpretable machine learning: systems that provide explanation for their outputs. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: “Why Should I Trust You?”: Explaining the Predictions of Any Classifier
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Despite widespread adoption in NLP, machine learning models remain mostly black boxes. top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: While neural networks have been successfully applied to many NLP tasks the resulting vectorbased models are very difficult to interpret. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Grad-CAM: Visual Explanations from Deep Networks via Gradient-Based Localization
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: We propose a technique for producing `visual explanations' for decisions from a large class of Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based models, making them more transparent. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Representation Engineering: A Top-Down Approach to AI Transparency
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: In this paper, we identify and characterize the emerging area of representation engineering (RepE), an approach to enhancing the transparency of AI systems that draws on insights from cognitive neuroscience. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Visualizing and Understanding Recurrent Networks
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs), and specifically a variant with Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), are enjoying renewed interest as a result of successful applications in a wide range of machine learning problems that involve sequential data. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: GLUE: A Multi-Task Benchmark and Analysis Platform for Natural Language Understanding
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Human ability to understand language is general, flexible, and robust. In contrast, most NLU models above the word level are designed for a specific task and struggle with out-of-domain data. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: In this work, we develop and release Llama 2, a collection of pretrained and fine-tuned large language models (LLMs) ranging in scale from 7 billion to 70 billion parameters. Our fine-tuned LLMs, called Llama 2-Chat, are optimized for dialogue use cases. Our models outperform open-source chat models on most benchmarks

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Inference-Time Intervention: Eliciting Truthful Answers from a Language Model
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: We introduce Inference-Time Intervention (ITI), a technique designed to enhance the "truthfulness" of large language models (LLMs). top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Analysis Methods in Neural Language Processing: A Survey
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Abstract The field of natural language processing has seen impressive progress in recent years, with neural network models replacing many of the traditional systems. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Transformer Feed-Forward Layers Are Key-Value Memories
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Feed-forward layers constitute two-thirds of a transformer model's parameters, yet their role in the network remains under-explored. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Fine-grained Analysis of Sentence Embeddings Using Auxiliary Prediction Tasks
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: There is a lot of research interest in encoding variable length sentences into fixed length vectors, in a way that preserves the sentence meanings. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: What do you learn from context? Probing for sentence structure in contextualized word representations
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Contextualized representation models such as ELMo (Peters et al., 2018a) and BERT (Devlin et al., 2018) have recently achieved state-of-the-art results on a diverse array of downstream NLP tasks. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Understanding deep image representations by inverting them
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Image representations, from SIFT and Bag of Visual Words to Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), are a crucial component of almost any image understanding system. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Learning Important Features Through Propagating Activation Differences
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: The purported "black box" nature of neural networks is a barrier to adoption in applications where interpretability is essential. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: While the Transformer architecture has become the de-facto standard for natural language processing tasks, its applications to computer vision remain limited. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Interpretability Beyond Feature Attribution: Quantitative Testing with Concept Activation Vectors (TCAV)
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: The interpretation of deep learning models is a challenge due to their size, complexity, and often opaque internal state. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Learning Deep Features for Discriminative Localization
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: In this work, we revisit the global average pooling layer proposed in [13], and shed light on how it explicitly enables the convolutional neural network (CNN) to have remarkable localization ability despite being trained on imagelevel labels. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Towards Automated Circuit Discovery for Mechanistic Interpretability
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Through considerable effort and intuition, several recent works have reverse-engineered nontrivial behaviors of transformer models. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Eliciting Latent Predictions from Transformers with the Tuned Lens
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: We analyze transformers from the perspective of iterative inference, seeking to understand how model predictions are refined layer by layer. To do so, we train an affine probe for each block in a frozen pretrained model, making it possible to decode every hidden state into a distribution over the vocabulary. Our method

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Amnesic Probing: Behavioral Explanation with Amnesic Counterfactuals
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Abstract A growing body of work makes use of probing in order to investigate the working of neural models, often considered black boxes. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Dissecting Recall of Factual Associations in Auto-Regressive Language Models
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Transformer-based language models (LMs) are known to capture factual knowledge in their parameters. While previous work looked into where factual associations are stored, only little is known about how they are retrieved internally during inference. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: A survey of methods for explaining black box models
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: In recent years, many accurate decision support systems have been constructed as black boxes, that is as systems that hide their internal logic to the user. This lack of explanation constitutes both a practical and an ethical issue. The literature reports many approaches aimed at overcoming this crucial weakness, somet

top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: The Geometry of Truth: Emergent Linear Structure in Large Language Model Representations of True/False Datasets
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have impressive capabilities, but are prone to outputting falsehoods. Recent work has developed techniques for inferring whether a LLM is telling the truth by training probes on the LLM's internal activations. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: What Does BERT Learn about the Structure of Language?
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: BERT is a recent language representation model that has surprisingly performed well in diverse language understanding benchmarks. top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: In the field of artificial intelligence, a combination of scale in data and model capacity enabled by unsupervised learning has led to major advances in representation learning and statistical generation. top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Language models are increasingly being deployed for general problem solving across a wide range of tasks, but are still confined to token-level, left-to-right decision-making processes during inference. top_by_in_corpus_cites.title: Memory-Based Model Editing at Scale
top_by_in_corpus_cites.abstract: Even the largest neural networks make errors, and once-correct predictions can become invalid as the world changes. Model editors make local updates to the behavior of base (pre-trained) models to inject updated knowledge or correct undesirable behaviors.
